clause_text: In the event the Lessee fails to pay the monthly rent reserved hereunder on the due date, the Lessor shall issue a written notice of demand to the Lessee at the said premises situated at Koramangala, Bengaluru, calling upon the Lessee to cure the default within fifteen (15) days from the date of receipt of such notice. Should the Lessee fail to remedy the arrears within the said period of fifteen days, the Lessor shall be at liberty to terminate this tenancy by serving a notice under Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, and thereafter proceed before the jurisdictional Small Causes Court at Bengaluru for an order of eviction under the Karnataka Rent Act, 1999. Nothing in this clause shall entitle the Lessor to re-enter the said premises otherwise than through due process of law.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Karnataka Rent Act 1999

clause_text: This tenancy being a month-to-month tenancy in respect of the said premises at Andheri (West), Mumbai, may be determined by either party by giving not less than fifteen (15) days' notice in writing expiring with the end of a month of the tenancy, in compliance with Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882. In the case of the Lessor, such notice shall also specify the ground of eviction recognised under the Maharashtra Rent Control Act, 1999, namely non-payment of rent, unlawful subletting, breach of a condition of tenancy, or bona fide requirement for personal occupation. No eviction shall be effected save in execution of a decree or order of the Competent Authority or the Court of Small Causes, Mumbai.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Maharashtra Rent Control Act 1999

clause_text: If the Licensee commits a breach of any material term of this Leave and Licence Agreement in respect of the said premises at Sector 62, Noida, the Licensor shall serve upon the Licensee a written notice specifying the breach and requiring the Licensee to rectify the same within a period of thirty (30) days from the receipt of the said notice. Upon failure of the Licensee to cure the breach within the said thirty-day period, the Licensor shall be entitled to terminate the Agreement by a further notice of fifteen (15) days in writing, whereupon the Licensee shall peacefully vacate and hand over quiet and vacant possession. The security deposit shall be refunded to the Licensee within thirty (30) days of handover, subject to deduction only of admitted arrears and documented damages beyond ordinary wear and tear.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Uttar Pradesh Regulation of Urban Premises Tenancy Act 2021

clause_text: The Lessor shall be entitled to seek eviction of the Lessee from the said premises situated at Salt Lake, Kolkata, only upon one or more of the grounds enumerated in Section 6 of the West Bengal Premises Tenancy Act, 2006, namely: (a) arrears of rent for a period exceeding three months; (b) unauthorised subletting or parting with possession; (c) user of the premises for a purpose other than that for which they were let; (d) causing material damage or structural alteration without consent; or (e) bona fide reasonable requirement of the Lessor for own occupation. Any such eviction shall be sought by due application to the Rent Controller having jurisdiction, and not otherwise.
indian_law: West Bengal Premises Tenancy Act 2006; Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106

clause_text: On expiry of the term hereby created, or on earlier determination of this Lease Deed in the manner herein provided, the Lessee shall peaceably deliver up to the Lessor quiet and vacant possession of the said demised premises at Banjara Hills, Hyderabad, together with all fixtures and fittings in the condition in which the Lessee received them, reasonable wear and tear excepted. In the event the Lessee holds over after the expiry of the term without the written consent of the Lessor, the tenancy shall be deemed to have been terminated, and the Lessor shall be entitled to approach the competent Court for recovery of possession in accordance with law. The statutory notice under Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882 shall be dispensed with only to the extent permitted by law, and not otherwise.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Telangana Buildings (Lease, Rent and Eviction) Control Act 1960

clause_text: In the event of non-payment of the monthly licence fee for two (2) consecutive months in respect of the said premises at Gachibowli, Hyderabad, the Licensor shall issue a written demand notice to the Licensee by registered post A.D. and by electronic mail. If the arrears together with interest at the agreed rate are not cleared within fifteen (15) days of receipt of such notice, the Licensor shall be entitled to terminate this Leave and Licence Agreement and seek recovery of possession before the Competent Authority under Section 24 of the Maharashtra Rent Control Act, 1999, or the applicable statute of the State where the premises are situated.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Maharashtra Rent Control Act 1999

clause_text: Either party may terminate this Rental Agreement in respect of the said premises at Defence Colony, New Delhi, by giving the other party a written notice of not less than thirty (30) days, notwithstanding the statutory minimum of fifteen (15) days prescribed under Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, which the parties agree to enhance by mutual consent for the convenience of planning alternate accommodation. On termination the Tenant shall hand over vacant possession, and the Landlord shall refund the interest-free security deposit within fifteen (15) days after adjusting bona fide arrears and documented damage, if any.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Delhi Rent Control Act 1958

clause_text: The Lessor covenants that eviction of the Lessee from the said premises at T. Nagar, Chennai, shall be sought only through the Rent Controller appointed under the Tamil Nadu Regulation of Rights and Responsibilities of Landlords and Tenants Act, 2017, and only upon the grounds specified in Section 21 of the said Act, including wilful default in payment of rent, subletting without written consent, use of the premises for a purpose other than the agreed purpose, and structural damage to the said premises. Forcible dispossession, disconnection of essential supplies, or change of locks without an order of the Rent Court is expressly prohibited and shall entitle the Lessee to seek injunctive relief under the Specific Relief Act, 1963.
indian_law: Tamil Nadu Regulation of Rights and Responsibilities of Landlords and Tenants Act 2017; Specific Relief Act 1963

clause_text: In the event the Lessor requires the said premises at Aundh, Pune, bona fide for the occupation of the Lessor or of any member of the Lessor's family dependent on the Lessor, the Lessor shall, not less than six (6) months prior to the expiry of the current year of the tenancy, deliver to the Lessee a written notice under Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, setting out the ground of bona fide requirement. The Lessee shall, on receipt of such notice, be entitled to a hearing before the Competent Authority, and eviction shall follow only upon a reasoned order holding that the bona fide requirement has been established.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Maharashtra Rent Control Act 1999

clause_text: Should the Lessee sublet, assign, or otherwise part with possession of the said premises at Indiranagar, Bengaluru, or any part thereof, without the prior written consent of the Lessor, the Lessor shall issue a notice in writing calling upon the Lessee to restore exclusive possession within thirty (30) days. On failure to comply, the Lessor may determine the tenancy by a further fifteen (15) days' notice under Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882 and apply to the Court of Small Causes, Bengaluru, for an order of eviction under the Karnataka Rent Act, 1999. The Lessee shall remain liable for use and occupation charges until handover of vacant possession.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Karnataka Rent Act 1999

clause_text: This Commercial Rental Agreement in respect of the office premises at Nariman Point, Mumbai, may be determined by the Licensor by giving the Licensee ninety (90) days' written notice in the event of default in payment of licence fee continuing beyond thirty (30) days after a written demand, or in the event of unauthorised change of user of the said premises. On termination, the Licensee shall vacate the premises and hand over the keys and access cards, and the Licensor shall refund the interest-free security deposit within forty-five (45) days, net of documented liabilities. The Licensor shall not be entitled to forcibly evict the Licensee except in execution of an order of the Court of Small Causes.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Maharashtra Rent Control Act 1999

clause_text: If the Tenant causes or permits to be caused any material damage to the said premises at Gomti Nagar, Lucknow, or any structural alteration thereto without the prior written consent of the Landlord, the Landlord shall serve a written notice specifying the breach and calling upon the Tenant to restore the premises to their original condition within forty-five (45) days. Upon failure to so restore, the Landlord may terminate this Rental Agreement by a fifteen (15) days' notice under Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882 and seek eviction and compensation before the Rent Authority under the Uttar Pradesh Regulation of Urban Premises Tenancy Act, 2021.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Uttar Pradesh Regulation of Urban Premises Tenancy Act 2021

clause_text: On lawful determination of this Leave and Licence Agreement in respect of the said premises at Powai, Mumbai, and delivery of vacant possession by the Licensee, the Licensor shall refund the interest-free security deposit of Rs. ___________ to the Licensee within a period of thirty (30) days. The Licensor shall be entitled to deduct from the said deposit only such amounts as are on account of (i) admitted arrears of licence fee, (ii) unpaid electricity, water and society maintenance charges up to the date of handover, and (iii) documented damage to the premises beyond ordinary wear and tear. Any dispute regarding the quantum of deduction shall be referred to the Competent Authority under the Maharashtra Rent Control Act, 1999.
indian_law: Maharashtra Rent Control Act 1999; Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106

clause_text: The Lessor shall not institute any suit or proceeding for eviction of the Lessee from the said premises at Jubilee Hills, Hyderabad, without first serving on the Lessee a notice in writing under Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, specifying the ground of termination and allowing the Lessee a period of thirty (30) days to show cause or remedy the default, as the case may admit. Any proceeding for recovery of possession shall lie before the Rent Controller constituted under the Telangana Buildings (Lease, Rent and Eviction) Control Act, 1960. Self-help remedies including change of locks, disconnection of electricity, or denial of access are expressly barred.
indian_law: Telangana Buildings (Lease, Rent and Eviction) Control Act 1960; Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106

clause_text: Where the Tenant has used the said premises at Saket, New Delhi, for a purpose other than that for which they were let, namely residential purposes, the Landlord shall be entitled to issue a notice in writing specifying the breach and granting the Tenant a period of thirty (30) days to restore user to the agreed purpose. On continued breach, the Landlord may terminate the tenancy under Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882 and apply to the Rent Controller under Section 14 of the Delhi Rent Control Act, 1958. The Tenant's statutory rights of hearing and appeal under the said Act shall not be prejudiced by this clause.
indian_law: Delhi Rent Control Act 1958 Section 14; Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106

clause_text: It is expressly agreed that in the event the Lessor desires to reconstruct the building in which the said premises at Alkapuri, Vadodara, are situate, the Lessor shall serve upon the Lessee a written notice of not less than six (6) months setting out the proposal for reconstruction and the necessity therefor. Eviction on the ground of reconstruction shall be sought only before the Rent Controller under the Gujarat Rent Act and the Lessee shall, on application, be entitled to be considered for reinduction into the reconstructed premises on reasonable terms.
indian_law: Gujarat Rent Act 2011; Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106

clause_text: Either party may terminate this Leave and Licence Agreement in respect of the said premises at HSR Layout, Bengaluru, by giving the other party a written notice of not less than two (2) months. The said notice period is agreed between the parties as exceeding the statutory minimum of fifteen days prescribed under Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, and shall not be reduced save by mutual written consent. On determination, the Licensee shall deliver vacant possession and the Licensor shall refund the deposit within thirty (30) days after lawful adjustments.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Karnataka Rent Act 1999

clause_text: If the Tenant is in arrears of rent in respect of the said premises at Vashi, Navi Mumbai, for a period exceeding two (2) months, the Landlord shall serve a written demand notice. If the arrears are not paid within fifteen (15) days from the receipt of the said notice, the Landlord shall be entitled to terminate the tenancy by serving a further notice of fifteen (15) days under Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, and to apply to the Competent Authority under the Maharashtra Rent Control Act, 1999 for an order of eviction. The Tenant may deposit the arrears with interest in court to defeat the ground of default as provided by law.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Maharashtra Rent Control Act 1999

clause_text: In the event of termination of this Lease Deed for any of the grounds set out in Clause ___ hereinabove, the Lessee shall be entitled to a cure period of fifteen (15) days from the date of receipt of the notice of breach from the Lessor in respect of the said premises at Adyar, Chennai, except in the case of arrears of rent where the cure period shall be fifteen (15) days from receipt of the demand notice. The Lessee's right to cure is expressly preserved in accordance with equitable principles and the Tamil Nadu Regulation of Rights and Responsibilities of Landlords and Tenants Act, 2017.
indian_law: Tamil Nadu Regulation of Rights and Responsibilities of Landlords and Tenants Act 2017; Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106

clause_text: On expiry of this Rental Agreement in respect of the said premises at Viman Nagar, Pune, or on earlier lawful determination, the Tenant shall hand over vacant possession together with the keys to the Landlord. The Landlord shall simultaneously refund the interest-free security deposit to the Tenant after adjusting only lawful dues. Should the Landlord fail to refund the deposit within thirty (30) days, the Tenant shall be entitled to interest at eighteen per cent (18%) per annum from the date of handover until actual payment, and to proceed before the Rent Authority for recovery.
indian_law: Maharashtra Rent Control Act 1999; Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106

clause_text: The Licensor shall not be entitled to re-enter or take possession of the said premises at Malviya Nagar, Jaipur, otherwise than in execution of an order duly passed by the Rent Tribunal under the Rajasthan Rent Control Act, 2001. Any attempt at forcible re-entry, change of locks, or denial of essential services shall constitute an actionable wrong, entitling the Licensee to seek an injunction under Section 38 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, together with damages and costs.
indian_law: Rajasthan Rent Control Act 2001; Specific Relief Act 1963 Section 38

clause_text: For the removal of doubt, it is clarified that nothing in this Lease Deed shall be construed as waiving the Lessee's statutory protections under the applicable Rent Control Act or the notice requirement under Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, in respect of the said premises at Kharghar, Navi Mumbai. The enumerated grounds of eviction herein are subject to and shall be read with the corresponding provisions of the Maharashtra Rent Control Act, 1999, and the procedures prescribed thereunder.
indian_law: Maharashtra Rent Control Act 1999; Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106

clause_text: In the event of the death of the Lessee, the tenancy in respect of the said premises at Bhowanipore, Kolkata, shall devolve upon the heirs of the Lessee as per the provisions of Section 2(g) of the West Bengal Premises Tenancy Act, 2006, and this Lease Deed shall not, by reason only of the demise of the Lessee, stand determined. The Lessor shall be entitled to seek eviction of the heirs only upon one of the statutory grounds and after due notice under Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882.
indian_law: West Bengal Premises Tenancy Act 2006; Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106

clause_text: The Lessor shall give the Lessee a written notice of fifteen (15) days under Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, expiring with the end of a month of the tenancy, to terminate the month-to-month tenancy of the said premises at Rajouri Garden, New Delhi. Such notice shall state the ground of termination and shall be served by registered post with acknowledgement due or by speed post at the last known address of the Lessee. Service by electronic mail shall be deemed good service only if the Lessee has in writing consented to such mode of service.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Delhi Rent Control Act 1958

clause_text: The Licensee covenants to use the said premises at Whitefield, Bengaluru, solely for residential purposes and for no other purpose whatsoever. Any unauthorised change of user including use for commercial, industrial, immoral, or illegal purposes shall constitute a ground for termination of this Leave and Licence Agreement. The Licensor shall, prior to terminating on such ground, issue a notice in writing giving the Licensee an opportunity of thirty (30) days to restore the user to the agreed purpose and to show cause against termination, in accordance with the Karnataka Rent Act, 1999.
indian_law: Karnataka Rent Act 1999; Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106

clause_text: Subject to the provisions of Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, and the relevant provisions of the Maharashtra Rent Control Act, 1999, this tenancy of the said premises at Bandra (East), Mumbai, may be determined by the Landlord on any of the following grounds: (a) arrears of rent for a period of fifteen days after lawful demand; (b) subletting without written consent; (c) use of premises for a purpose other than for which let; (d) causing nuisance or annoyance to neighbours; (e) structural damage; (f) bona fide personal requirement. The Landlord shall make out the ground before the Competent Authority.
indian_law: Maharashtra Rent Control Act 1999; Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106

clause_text: On determination of this Commercial Rental Agreement for the office premises at Cyber City, Gurugram, whether by efflux of time or by earlier notice, the Licensee shall vacate the said premises within the notice period and hand over peaceful possession to the Licensor. The Licensor shall refund the security deposit equivalent to three (3) months' rent within thirty (30) days of handover, subject to bona fide deductions. The Licensor acknowledges that eviction shall be sought only through the Rent Tribunal under the Haryana Urban (Control of Rent and Eviction) Act, 1973 or by suit before the competent civil court, and not through extra-judicial means.
indian_law: Haryana Urban (Control of Rent and Eviction) Act 1973; Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106

clause_text: Where the Tenant has, in respect of the said premises at Anna Nagar, Chennai, committed a wilful default in payment of rent for two (2) consecutive months, the Landlord may issue a notice under Section 21(2)(a) of the Tamil Nadu Regulation of Rights and Responsibilities of Landlords and Tenants Act, 2017, calling upon the Tenant to pay the arrears within one month. If the Tenant pays the arrears with interest within the said period, the Landlord shall not be entitled to seek eviction on the ground of default. Nothing herein shall preclude the Landlord from proceeding on any other statutory ground available.
indian_law: Tamil Nadu Regulation of Rights and Responsibilities of Landlords and Tenants Act 2017 Section 21

clause_text: In the event the Licensee fails to vacate the said premises at Kondhwa, Pune, on determination of this Leave and Licence Agreement, the Licensor shall be entitled to approach the Competent Authority under Section 24 of the Maharashtra Rent Control Act, 1999, for a summary order of possession. The Licensee shall, during the period of unauthorised occupation, be liable to pay double the agreed licence fee by way of compensation for use and occupation, without prejudice to any other remedy available to the Licensor in law.
indian_law: Maharashtra Rent Control Act 1999 Section 24; Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106

clause_text: The Lessor shall, in the event of breach of any covenant by the Lessee in respect of the said premises at Vasant Kunj, New Delhi, issue a notice in writing specifying the breach and granting the Lessee a reasonable period of not less than thirty (30) days to remedy the same. The Lessor shall be entitled to forfeit the lease and re-enter the premises only after obtaining an order of the Rent Controller under Section 14 of the Delhi Rent Control Act, 1958. The proviso to Section 111(g) of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, shall stand fully attracted.
indian_law: Delhi Rent Control Act 1958 Section 14; Transfer of Property Act 1882 Sections 106 and 111(g)

clause_text: Either party may terminate this year-to-year tenancy of the agricultural and residential premises at Village Morna, Greater Noida, by giving the other party a notice in writing of not less than six (6) months expiring with the end of a year of the tenancy, in accordance with Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882. The notice shall specify the grounds for termination in the case of notice by the Lessor, and eviction, if contested, shall be determined by the civil court having jurisdiction.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106

clause_text: The Licensor shall provide the Licensee of the said premises at Baner, Pune, with a written notice of sixty (60) days prior to the expiry of the term of this Leave and Licence Agreement, informing the Licensee whether the Licensor intends to renew the Agreement or to take possession of the premises. In the event of non-renewal, the Licensee shall peacefully hand over possession on expiry of the term, and the security deposit shall be refunded within fifteen (15) days thereafter after adjusting documented dues. Recovery of possession, if resisted, shall be through the Competent Authority and not otherwise.
indian_law: Maharashtra Rent Control Act 1999; Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106

clause_text: If the Tenant, his family members, or guests cause nuisance or annoyance to the neighbours or to the persons occupying the said building at Electronic City, Bengaluru, the Landlord shall issue a written notice describing the conduct complained of and calling upon the Tenant to desist therefrom within fifteen (15) days. On continuation of such conduct, the Landlord may terminate the tenancy under Section 27(2)(g) of the Karnataka Rent Act, 1999 by serving a notice under Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, and seek eviction before the Court of Small Causes.
indian_law: Karnataka Rent Act 1999 Section 27; Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106

clause_text: On termination of this Lease Deed in respect of the said premises at Fort, Mumbai, whether by efflux of time or by earlier determination in accordance with these presents, the parties shall together prepare a joint inspection report of the premises. The Lessor shall refund the security deposit to the Lessee within thirty (30) days of handover, after deducting only the amounts mutually agreed in the said joint inspection report or finally adjudicated by the Competent Authority under the Maharashtra Rent Control Act, 1999.
indian_law: Maharashtra Rent Control Act 1999; Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106

clause_text: It is hereby agreed that in the event the said premises at Model Town, New Delhi, are required bona fide by the Lessor for the occupation of the Lessor or of a member of the Lessor's family dependent on the Lessor, the Lessor shall apply to the Rent Controller under Section 14(1)(e) of the Delhi Rent Control Act, 1958, for recovery of possession. The Lessee shall be entitled to contest the application on the grounds of absence of bona fide need, availability of alternative suitable accommodation with the Lessor, and disproportionate hardship, as provided under the said Act.
indian_law: Delhi Rent Control Act 1958 Section 14(1)(e); Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106

clause_text: The Lessor and the Lessee hereby agree that no notice of termination under Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, in respect of the said premises at Hebbal, Bengaluru, shall be effective unless served in writing by registered post with acknowledgement due or by personal delivery against written acknowledgement. Service by SMS, WhatsApp, or other informal means shall not be valid service. On receipt of a valid notice, the Lessee shall have the full notice period for making alternative arrangements, and the Lessor shall not interfere with the Lessee's possession during that period.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Karnataka Rent Act 1999

clause_text: In the event the Licensee commits any breach whatsoever of any term, condition, or covenant of this Leave and Licence Agreement in respect of the said premises at Bandra-Kurla Complex, Mumbai, the Licensor shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement forthwith by a notice of seven (7) days in writing, notwithstanding any provision of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882 to the contrary. The determination of what constitutes a "breach" shall lie in the sole and absolute discretion of the Licensor, whose decision shall be final and binding on the Licensee. Time shall be of the essence of this clause.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106

clause_text: Any delay or default by the Tenant in payment of the monthly rent for the said premises at Greater Kailash-I, New Delhi, beyond the fifth (5th) day of the month shall automatically entitle the Landlord, without the requirement of any notice or demand, to terminate this tenancy forthwith and to take back possession of the premises. The security deposit shall stand forfeited in full on such termination as pre-estimated damages, and no cure period shall be granted.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106

clause_text: The Licensor may, in its sole and absolute discretion, terminate this Leave and Licence Agreement in respect of the said premises at Viman Nagar, Pune, on seven (7) days' written notice for any "material breach" by the Licensee, the determination of which shall be made solely by the Licensor and shall not be open to question or review. On such termination, the Licensee shall vacate the premises within the notice period, failing which the Licensor shall be entitled to treat the Licensee as a trespasser.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Maharashtra Rent Control Act 1999

clause_text: Upon any violation of any term of this Rental Agreement in respect of the said premises at Sector 15, Chandigarh, howsoever trivial or technical, the Landlord shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement with immediate effect and the Tenant shall vacate the premises within seven (7) days. The full security deposit shall stand forfeited automatically as agreed liquidated damages, without the need for quantification or proof of actual loss.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106

clause_text: If the Tenant is late in payment of rent for the said premises at Kalyani Nagar, Pune, by even a single day, the Landlord shall levy a penalty of Rs. 500 per day, and if such delay exceeds seven (7) days, the Landlord may terminate the tenancy forthwith by written notice and re-take possession. No opportunity to cure shall be granted in respect of payment defaults, time being the essence of the contract.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Maharashtra Rent Control Act 1999

clause_text: The Licensor reserves the absolute right to terminate this Leave and Licence Agreement for the said commercial premises at MG Road, Gurugram, at any time by giving seven (7) days' written notice without assigning any reason whatsoever. The Licensee expressly agrees that such termination shall not give rise to any claim for damages, compensation, or loss of business, and that the security deposit shall be refunded net of arrears only, without interest.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106

clause_text: In the event of any breach by the Lessee of any covenant whatsoever in respect of the said premises at Velachery, Chennai, the Lessor shall be entitled to terminate this Lease Deed forthwith on seven (7) days' notice. The Lessee shall have no right to be heard, no right to cure the alleged breach, and no right to challenge the Lessor's determination of breach before any forum other than through a suit for damages after the Lessor has recovered possession.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106

clause_text: The Licensee agrees that on termination of this Leave and Licence Agreement for the said premises at Andheri (East), Mumbai, for any reason attributable to the Licensee, including but not limited to default in payment of licence fee, unauthorised guests, noise, or violation of society rules, the entire security deposit shall stand automatically forfeited to the Licensor as agreed pre-estimated damages, without any obligation on the Licensor to account for or refund any portion thereof.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Maharashtra Rent Control Act 1999

clause_text: The Landlord shall be the sole judge of whether any act or omission of the Tenant constitutes a breach of the said premises at Hiranandani Estate, Thane, and the Landlord's written determination of breach shall be conclusive and binding on the Tenant. On such determination, the tenancy shall stand terminated on seven (7) days' notice, and the Tenant shall not be entitled to contest the determination in any forum whatsoever.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Maharashtra Rent Control Act 1999

clause_text: If the Tenant keeps any pet, hosts any overnight guest, smokes on the premises, or consumes non-vegetarian food at the said premises at Paldi, Ahmedabad, the Landlord shall treat the same as a material breach entitling the Landlord to terminate this tenancy by a notice of seven (7) days. The security deposit shall be forfeited, and the Tenant shall have no right to cure the breach by removal of the pet, guest, or discontinuation of the conduct complained of.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106

clause_text: The Licensor shall have the unqualified right to terminate this Leave and Licence Agreement for the said premises at Koramangala 4th Block, Bengaluru, with seven (7) days' notice in the event of a single instance of late payment of licence fee or of any complaint received from any neighbour or any third party. The Licensor shall not be required to verify the complaint or to provide a hearing to the Licensee prior to termination.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Karnataka Rent Act 1999

clause_text: The Tenant agrees that on termination of this Rental Agreement for the said premises at Gomti Nagar Extension, Lucknow, on any ground, the entire security deposit of Rs. ___________ shall stand forfeited as non-refundable termination charges, and the Landlord shall be under no obligation whatsoever to render account or refund any part thereof, regardless of the actual damage, if any, caused by the Tenant.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106

clause_text: In the event the Licensee fails to pay the licence fee for even one (1) month on the due date in respect of the said premises at Jayanagar, Bengaluru, the Licensor may terminate this Agreement on seven (7) days' written notice. The Licensee shall have no right to tender the arrears after the said notice is issued, and tender of arrears after the notice shall not defeat the termination. Time shall be of the strictest essence of this Agreement.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Karnataka Rent Act 1999

clause_text: The Landlord shall have the right to terminate this tenancy in respect of the said premises at Sector 50, Noida, on a seven (7) day notice if in the Landlord's sole opinion the conduct of the Tenant is "unsuitable" for the society, building, or neighbourhood, without any requirement to specify particulars or furnish evidence of such unsuitable conduct. The Tenant waives any right to challenge the Landlord's subjective opinion in this regard.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Uttar Pradesh Regulation of Urban Premises Tenancy Act 2021

clause_text: On the Licensor serving a notice of termination on any ground whatsoever in respect of the said premises at DLF Phase 2, Gurugram, the Licensee shall vacate within seven (7) days, failing which the Licensor shall be entitled to levy hold-over charges at four (4) times the agreed licence fee in addition to forfeiture of the security deposit. No opportunity to cure or to seek extension shall be available to the Licensee.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Haryana Urban (Control of Rent and Eviction) Act 1973

clause_text: The Tenant shall not raise any dispute or objection whatsoever in respect of the termination of this Agreement for the said premises at Kothrud, Pune, and any such dispute or objection shall automatically be treated as an additional ground of breach. The Landlord's decision to terminate, once communicated, shall be final and the Tenant shall vacate within seven (7) days regardless of any pending discussion, representation, or dispute.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Maharashtra Rent Control Act 1999

clause_text: For the said commercial premises at Nehru Place, New Delhi, the Licensor shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement at any time by a seven (7) days' written notice, and the Licensee shall thereupon vacate the premises and remove all its goods, fixtures and fittings. Any goods, fixtures or fittings not removed within the notice period shall be deemed abandoned and shall become the property of the Licensor without payment of compensation.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106

Ltd. at Kolkata and Sneha Banerjee, engaged in the capacity of a professional in the e-commerce sector, default in payment of monthly rent or licence fee beyond the statutory grace period shall entitle the Lessor to determine the tenancy in accordance with the Rajasthan Rent Control Act, 2001 read with Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882.  In the case of the Lessor, the notice of termination shall also specify the ground of eviction recognised under the Rajasthan Rent Control Act, 2001, namely non-payment of rent, unlawful subletting, breach of a condition of tenancy, or bona fide requirement for personal occupation.  This clause shall be read in conjunction with the Rajasthan Rent Control Act, 2001 and the provisions of Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, and shall be construed so as to give full effect to both statutes.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Rajasthan Rent Control Act, 2001

Ltd. at its BPO/KPO operations at Mumbai, any breach of the terms of this leave and license agreement shall entitle the Lessor to issue a cure notice of thirty (30) days to the Lessee, and upon failure to cure within the said period, the Lessor shall be at liberty to terminate the tenancy by further notice in writing.  The Lessor shall give the Lessee at least 45 days' written notice to remedy any alleged breach before invoking the eviction machinery under the Rajasthan Rent Control Act, 2001, and the Lessor shall act in good faith in assessing the cure.  This clause shall be read in conjunction with the Rajasthan Rent Control Act, 2001 and the provisions of Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, and shall be construed so as to give full effect to both statutes.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Rajasthan Rent Control Act, 2001

clause_text: In respect of the commercial lease deed between Pratham Chemicals Pvt. Ltd. (having its principal place of business at Kochi) and Kunal Desai engaged in the SaaS product development division, in the event the Licensee fails to vacate the said premises at Infopark, Kochi, on the expiry of the term or on the earlier determination of this commercial lease deed, the Licensor shall be entitled to initiate proceedings for eviction before the jurisdictional court at Kochi.  Upon failure of the Lessee to remedy the breach or arrears within the notice period, the Lessor shall be at liberty to terminate the tenancy by serving a further notice under Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, and thereafter proceed before the jurisdictional Small Causes Court or Rent Controller at Kochi for an order of eviction.  Any dispute as to the ground of eviction or the quantum of deduction from the security deposit shall be referred to the jurisdictional Rent Controller at Kochi in accordance with the Kerala Buildings (Lease and Rent Control) Act, 1965.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Kerala Buildings (Lease and Rent Control) Act, 1965

clause_text: The Lessor may terminate this lease deed on any of the grounds enumerated in Section 12 of the West Bengal Premises Tenancy Act, 1997, being the grounds of non-payment of rent, unlawful subletting, change of user, breach of a condition of tenancy or bona fide requirement for personal occupation.  In the case of the Lessor, the notice of termination shall also specify the ground of eviction recognised under the West Bengal Premises Tenancy Act, 1997, namely non-payment of rent, unlawful subletting, breach of a condition of tenancy, or bona fide requirement for personal occupation.  The Lessor shall, following eviction, issue to the Lessee a detailed statement of the security deposit adjustment, and any balance remaining shall be refunded by NEFT/RTGS within the time stipulated.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; West Bengal Premises Tenancy Act, 1997

clause_text: In the context of the services rendered by Aarti Verma to Virat Foods Pvt. Ltd., a company engaged in consultancy with its registered office at Bengaluru, the parties have agreed that In the event the Lessee fails to pay the monthly rent reserved hereunder on the due date, the Lessor shall issue a written notice of demand calling upon the Lessee to cure the default within fifteen (15) days of receipt of such notice in respect of the said premises situated at AB Road, Indore.  The Lessee shall be entitled to a reasonable opportunity of being heard before any order of eviction is passed, and the Lessor shall not attempt to obtain possession by self-help or by forcible re-entry in violation of Section 6 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963.  This clause shall be read in conjunction with the Madhya Pradesh Accommodation Control Act, 1961 and the provisions of Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, and shall be construed so as to give full effect to both statutes.

clause_text: It is hereby recorded that with reference to this leave and license agreement executed between Kairos Digital Pvt. Ltd. at Pune and Rohit Verma, engaged in the capacity of a professional in the pharmaceuticals sector, this tenancy being a month-to-month tenancy in respect of the said premises at Dwarka, Delhi, may be determined by either party by giving not less than fifteen (15) days' notice in writing expiring with the end of a month of the tenancy, in compliance with Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882.  No eviction shall be effected save in execution of a decree or order of the Competent Authority or the Court of Small Causes at Delhi, and until such decree or order the Lessee shall continue to enjoy quiet possession of the said premises. clause_text: With effect from the date of execution of this rental agreement at Ahmedabad between Vaayu Finserv LLP and Rhea Yadav, any alleged breach of the terms of this rental agreement, including non-payment of rent, unauthorised use of the premises or any other breach in the Lessor's sole assessment, shall entitle the Lessor to automatic forfeiture of the security deposit of Rs. 180,000/-.  The Lessor shall be entitled to recover an amount equivalent to three (3) months' rent as damages for illegal overholding, payable from the date of expiry of the notice period until the date of vacation.  The Lessee shall remove all possessions from the said premises within seventy-two (72) hours of receipt of the termination notice, failing which the Lessor may remove and store them at the Lessee's cost, without liability for any loss or damage.

The Lessee shall not be entitled to raise before any forum the defence that the notice of eviction was not served for a reasonable period or that the ground of eviction does not fall within the Tamil Nadu Regulation of Rights and Responsibilities of Landlords and Tenants Act, 2017.  The Lessee shall remove all possessions from the said premises within seventy-two (72) hours of receipt of the termination notice, failing which the Lessor may remove and store them at the Lessee's cost, without liability for any loss or damage.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Tamil Nadu Regulation of Rights and Responsibilities of Landlords and Tenants Act, 2017

clause_text: It is hereby recorded that with reference to this lease deed executed between Pragati Studios Pvt. Ltd. at Bhubaneswar and Anjali Joshi, engaged in the capacity of a professional in the fintech sector, in the event of any breach of this lease deed in respect of the said premises at Thane, Mumbai, the Lessor shall serve a seven (7) day notice on the Lessee, failing cure of which the Lessor shall be entitled to forfeit the entire security deposit and terminate the tenancy forthwith.  The security deposit of Rs. 120,000/- shall stand automatically forfeited on the occurrence of any breach, and the Lessee shall not be entitled to any adjustment of the deposit against arrears of rent or damages.  Any challenge by the Lessee to the eviction notice before the Rent Controller, Small Causes Court or High Court shall entitle the Lessor to recover legal costs on a full indemnity basis in addition to other remedies.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Maharashtra Rent Control Act, 1999

clause_text: This provision shall govern the relationship between Chandra Systems Pvt. Ltd., a company incorporated under the laws of India and carrying on business in retail at Hyderabad, and Lakshmi Naidu, and The Lessor reserves the unilateral right to determine, at its sole discretion, whether any conduct of the Lessee constitutes breach of this leave and license agreement, and the Lessor's determination shall be final and binding notwithstanding any contrary evidence.  The Lessor's right to re-enter shall include the right to the assistance of the local police for peaceful removal of the Lessee, and the Lessee shall not resist such re-entry.  The Lessee shall remove all possessions from the said premises within seventy-two (72) hours of receipt of the termination notice, failing which the Lessor may remove and store them at the Lessee's cost, without liability for any loss or damage.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Rajasthan Rent Control Act, 2001

The Lessee shall not be entitled to receive the security deposit of Rs. 220,000/- or any part thereof on eviction under this clause, and such deposit shall stand forfeited to the Lessor as absolute liquidated damages.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Kerala Buildings (Lease and Rent Control) Act, 1965

clause_text: Pursuant to the engagement of Jignesh Mishra by Veda Networks Pvt. Ltd. at its automobile manufacturing operations at Jaipur, the Lessor shall be entitled to forcibly remove the Lessee and the Lessee's goods and possessions from the said premises at any time upon the Lessee's alleged default, and the Lessee waives any claim for trespass, damage or interference with possession.  The Lessor's determination of the ground of eviction shall be final and binding, and the Telangana Buildings (Lease, Rent and Eviction) Control Act, 1960 shall be deemed inapplicable to the tenancy, notwithstanding the express language of that statute.  The Lessee shall not be entitled to receive the security deposit of Rs. 400,000/- or any part thereof on eviction under this clause, and such deposit shall stand forfeited to the Lessor as absolute liquidated damages.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Telangana Buildings (Lease, Rent and Eviction) Control Act, 1960

clause_text: This clause shall apply to the engagement of Tanvi Mukherjee with Ganga Finserv LLP at its logistics establishment at Coimbatore, and Notwithstanding Section 6 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, which bars dispossession save by due process of law, the Lessor shall have the right of physical re-entry on the said premises without any court order, on the occurrence of any alleged default.  The Lessee shall indemnify the Lessor against any legal costs incurred by the Lessor in resisting any claim brought by the Lessee under the Kerala Buildings (Lease and Rent Control) Act, 1965 or the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, on a full indemnity basis.  The Lessor may, at its discretion, initiate criminal proceedings under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 for criminal trespass against the Lessee who fails to vacate on demand, and the Lessee's failure to vacate shall constitute the mens rea for such offence.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Kerala Buildings (Lease and Rent Control) Act, 1965

clause_text: This clause shall apply to the engagement of Yogesh Chauhan with Srinivasa Consulting LLP at its information technology services establishment at Hyderabad, and The Lessor shall be entitled, at its sole discretion, to enter the said premises at Sohna Road, Gurugram, change the locks and dispossess the Lessee without the necessity of judicial process, notwithstanding the provisions of the Specific Relief Act, 1963.  The Lessee shall indemnify the Lessor against any legal costs incurred by the Lessor in resisting any claim brought by the Lessee under the Haryana Urban (Control of Rent and Eviction) Act, 1973 or the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, on a full indemnity basis.  This waiver and authorisation of self-help is given as consideration for the grant of tenancy, and shall bind the Lessee notwithstanding any judicial pronouncement holding that such waiver is void as against public policy.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Haryana Urban (Control of Rent and Eviction) Act, 1973

clause_text: The parties being Tatva Mobility Pvt. Ltd., a company registered under the Companies Act, 2013 having its corporate office at Chennai, and Vidya Kulkarni, have agreed that The Lessee hereby waives the notice required under Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882 in respect of the said premises at Malad, Mumbai, and agrees that the Lessor may determine this tenancy with immediate effect and re-enter the premises forthwith.  The Lessor's determination of the ground of eviction shall be final and binding, and the Maharashtra Rent Control Act, 1999 shall be deemed inapplicable to the tenancy, notwithstanding the express language of that statute.  The parties specifically record that the bar on recovery of possession without due process under the Specific Relief Act, 1963 shall not be invoked by the Lessee in any proceeding, and any such invocation shall be a breach of this leave and license agreement.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Maharashtra Rent Control Act, 1999

clause_text: This provision shall govern the relationship between Indigo Biosciences Pvt. Ltd., a company incorporated under the laws of India and carrying on business in insurance at Indore, and Kirti Naidu, and Notwithstanding Section 6 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, which bars dispossession save by due process of law, the Lessor shall have the right of physical re-entry on the said premises without any court order, on the occurrence of any alleged default.  The Lessee acknowledges that self-help by the Lessor, including changing locks, disconnecting utilities or physically removing the Lessee, shall not be actionable as trespass, nuisance or conversion.  The parties specifically record that the bar on recovery of possession without due process under the Specific Relief Act, 1963 shall not be invoked by the Lessee in any proceeding, and any such invocation shall be a breach of this commercial lease deed.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Rajasthan Rent Control Act, 2001

clause_text: In the context of the services rendered by Nisha Mukherjee to Bharat Technologies Pvt. Ltd., a company engaged in fintech with its registered office at Hyderabad, the parties have agreed that On demand by the Lessor at any time during the term of this leave and license agreement, the Lessee shall immediately vacate the said premises at Sector 135, Noida, without the necessity of any notice under Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882 or the Uttar Pradesh Regulation of Urban Premises Tenancy Act, 2021.  The Lessee shall indemnify the Lessor against any legal costs incurred by the Lessor in resisting any claim brought by the Lessee under the Uttar Pradesh Regulation of Urban Premises Tenancy Act, 2021 or the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, on a full indemnity basis. clause_text: This clause shall apply to the engagement of Aishwarya Ghosh with Aarogya Systems Pvt. Ltd. at its banking and financial services establishment at Kolkata, and The Lessor shall be entitled to forcibly remove the Lessee and the Lessee's goods and possessions from the said premises at any time upon the Lessee's alleged default, and the Lessee waives any claim for trespass, damage or interference with possession.  The Lessee expressly waives all protections under the Tamil Nadu Regulation of Rights and Responsibilities of Landlords and Tenants Act, 2017, including the right to a cure period, the right to contest the ground of eviction and the right to seek relief against forfeiture under Section 114 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882.  This waiver and authorisation of self-help is given as consideration for the grant of tenancy, and shall bind the Lessee notwithstanding any judicial pronouncement holding that such waiver is void as against public policy.
indian_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 106; Tamil Nadu Regulation of Rights and Responsibilities of Landlords and Tenants Act, 2017
